summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the 'Planted' series by addressing the common spiritual drift many believers experience, where initial passion for God can fade over time. He explained this drift using Jesus's parable of the sower from Matthew 13, emphasizing that the single most important factor for spiritual health and growth is the soil—the spiritual environment and inputs—in which a person is planted. The core message was a call to be 'Planted in God's Word,' arguing that true knowledge of God and resilience in life's storms come only from being deeply rooted in Scripture. Pastor Travis warned against being 'tumbleweed Christians' with shallow roots, who are easily uprooted by pressure, or whose faith is choked out by the worries of life and the deceitfulness of wealth. He passionately urged the congregation, especially the men, to move from being spectators to participators through discipline, routine, and clearing out distractions to cultivate a life that bears lasting fruit. The sermon concluded with an invitation for salvation and a prayer for God to examine and purify the soil of each person's heart.
mainPoints: If you don't know the Word of God, you don't truly know God; a shallow understanding leaves you vulnerable to the enemy's distortions.
Shallow spiritual roots cannot handle real life; without depth, faith collapses under pressure, producing unstable 'tumbleweed Christians.'
What you allow to grow in your life—worries, greed, distractions—can choke out God's Word; spiritual fruit requires intentionally cultivating space for God.

versesMentioned.text: Then he told them many things in parables, saying: 'A farmer went out to sow his seed. As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path, and the birds came and ate it up. Some fell on rocky places, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly, because the soil was shallow. But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched, and they withered because they had no root. Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the plants. Still other seed fell on good soil, where it produced a crop—a hundred, sixty or thirty times what was sown.'

When anyone hears the message about the kingdom and does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown in their heart. This is the seed sown along the path. The seed falling on rocky ground refers to someone who hears the word and at once receives it with joy. But since they have no root, they last only a short time. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, they quickly fall away. The seed falling among the thorns refers to someone who hears the word, but the worries of this life and the deceitfulness of wealth choke the word, making it unfruitful. But the seed falling on good soil refers to someone who hears the word and understands it. This is the one who produces a crop, yielding a hundred, sixty or thirty times what was sown.'

Blessed is the one who does not walk in step with the wicked or stand in the way that sinners take or sit in the company of mockers, but whose delight is in the law of the Lord, and who meditates on his law day and night. That person is like a tree planted by streams of water, which yields its fruit in season and whose leaf does not wither—whatever they do prospers.

And the Lord God commanded the man, 'You are free to eat from any tree in the garden; but you must not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, for when you eat from it you will certainly die.'

Now the serpent was more crafty than any of the wild animals the Lord God had made. He said to the woman, 'Did God really say, "You must not eat from any tree in the garden"?' The woman said to the serpent, 'We may eat fruit from the trees in the garden, but God did say, "You must not eat fruit from the tree that is in the middle of the garden, and you must not touch it, or you will die."' 'You will not certainly die,' the serpent said to the woman. 'For God knows that when you eat from it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing good and evil.'

Have nothing to do with godless myths and old wives’ tales; rather, train yourself to be godly. For physical training is of some value, but godliness has value for all things, holding promise for both the present life and the life to come.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 1:1-3 (NIV) - 'Blessed is the one...whose delight is in the law of the Lord...That person is like a tree planted by streams of water...'
Monday.action: Identify one distraction (e.g., phone use, worry) and intentionally set it aside for 15 minutes to read the Bible.
Monday.prayer: God, show me the joy of being planted in Your Word. Help me delight in it today.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 13:19 (NIV) - 'When anyone hears the message...and does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown...'
Tuesday.action: Read a short passage (e.g., Psalm 1) twice. The second time, write down one question you have about it.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, give me understanding. Protect Your word in my heart from being snatched away.

Wednesday.scripture: Matthew 13:20-21 (NIV) - 'The seed falling on rocky ground refers to someone who hears the word...But since they have no root, they last only a short time.'
Wednesday.action: Spend 5 minutes in quiet prayer, asking God to show you an area where your faith feels shallow. Commit to learning one thing about that topic from the Bible this week.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, dig my roots deep. Anchor me in You so I won't fall away when trouble comes.

Thursday.scripture: Matthew 13:22 (NIV) - 'The seed falling among the thorns refers to someone who hears the word, but the worries of this life...choke the word, making it unfruitful.'
Thursday.action: Make a simple list of the top 3 'worries of this life' occupying your mind. Pray over each one and consciously surrender it to God.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, I surrender my worries to You. Clear the thorns from my heart so Your word can bear fruit.

Friday.action: Establish one small, repeatable spiritual routine (e.g., reading one verse before breakfast, praying in the car). Do it today.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me the discipline and desire to train myself for godliness. Make my faith strong through consistency.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 1:3 (NIV) - 'That person is like a tree planted by streams of water, which yields its fruit in season...'
Saturday.action: Reflect on your week. Identify one way you can actively serve or encourage someone in your church or community as an act of fruitful faith.
Saturday.prayer: God, as I am planted in You, let my life yield good fruit. Use me to bless someone else this weekend.
